pattern: When two lattice paths only overlap column by column, tracking each walker's exit row per column turns an entangled two-path search into a clean interval-overlap DP.
hints: Trying to move both players in lockstep breaks down because shared cells need not be reached at the same step — describe the paths by shape instead.
Within any single column, each player's cells form one contiguous vertical run, fixed entirely by the row where they entered and the row where they leave.
Let the state after each column be both players' exit rows; the shared contribution of the next column is just the overlap of two intervals, computable from column prefix sums.

edge_inputs.description: all-negative grid: the best answer is still negative because sharing is forced
wide short grid — long horizontal overlap possible
tall narrow grid — vertical overlap dominates
zeros everywhere except one forced corner decision
best shared region sits mid-grid, not on either diagonal

stress.description: random small rectangles (rows x cols capped near 144 cells) with full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(2, 12)
    n = rng.randint(2, max(2, min(size, 144) // m))
    grid = [[rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
